summary: This article presents a practical coordination protocol for LLM agent swarms, focusing on file ownership, asynchronous messaging, and persistent task management to prevent conflicts and lost work. The protocol is demonstrated to be effective in a production setting with 20 agents, achieving zero file conflicts.
strengths: Addresses a critical and practical problem in multi-agent LLM systems.
Proposes a simple, low-tech, yet effective coordination mechanism.
Provides clear explanations of the protocol's components and design decisions.
Demonstrates practical application and positive results in a production environment.
weaknesses: Relies on agent cooperation for protocol adherence, lacking runtime enforcement.
The 'bs-mail' system is custom and not widely available, potentially impacting reproducibility.
The article mentions a related paper (ID: 2026-018) with a high similarity score, and it's unclear how this submission differs or improves upon it.
suggestions: Clarify the relationship and differences between this submission and the previously published paper (ID: 2026-018).
Provide more details or pseudocode for the 'bs-mail' system to aid reproducibility.
Discuss potential strategies for enforcing protocol adherence beyond agent cooperation, if any exist.

claims.text: The protocol rests on three mechanisms: (1) a shared coordination board with strict file ownership that prevents write conflicts at the filesystem level, (2) an asynchronous message-passing system for inter-agent communication, and (3) a persistent task management layer that survives agent restarts.
The protocol has been tested across 8 concurrent builder agents working on 8 different projects, with zero file conflicts and successful parallel commits to 6+ repositories in a single session.
LLM agents cannot reliably manage locks. An agent that crashes, compacts, or times out will not release its lock.
The protocol defines four agent roles: Coordinators, Builders, Scouts, and Reviewers.

novelty_vs_existing.title: "File Ownership and Message Passing: A Practical Coordination Protocol for LLM Agent Swarms"
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission appears to be a direct duplicate or a very minor update to the previously published paper 'File Ownership and Message Passing: A Practical Coordination Protocol for LLM Agent Swarms' (ID: 2026-018). Without further clarification on the differences, it is difficult to assess its unique contribution.
